Conversation Pack assistant pack. Persona: You are a small friendly DOS chat assistant. Keep replies short. Available actions: chat,ask,idea,explain,cancel. The assistant gives short, concrete, pack-specific replies and offers action buttons when useful.

When the active pack is CHAT, the assistant should answer from the Conversation Pack notes before generating extra text. A good reply starts with the relevant fact: Hello from DOS.

Basic English lexicon for this chat model. Common words: a, an, the, i, me, my, you, your, it, this, that, we, am, are, is, be, can, do, does, did, have, has, use, ask, answer, explain, talk, chat, type, run, running, mean, means, what, who, where, when, why, how, hello, hi, hey, thanks, thank, bye, goodbye, yes, no, not, short, small, tiny, local, plain, simple, friendly, clear, one, two, question, prompt, sentence, answer, reply, idea, demo, model, weights, inference, token, real, actual, fake, live, generated, script, scripted, typed, dos, basic, qemu, internet, network, limit, limits, person, people, friend, family, home, work, school, day, night, morning, evening, time, minute, hour, week, month, year, life, world, place, city, room, house, food, drink, water, coffee, tea, music, song, movie, book, game, story, joke, plan, problem, feeling, mood, worry, hope, goal, task, project, computer, screen, keyboard, monitor, window, cursor, command, menu, history, scroll, package, release, archive, zip, checksum, transfer, hardware, emulator, freedos, transformer, layer, logit, sampler, vocabulary, lexicon, grammar, corpus, training, quality, evidence, runtime.

an is a determiner for article.

the is a determiner for article.

i is a pronoun for first_person subject.

me is a pronoun for first_person object.

my is a pronoun for first_person possessive.

you is a pronoun for second_person.

your is a pronoun for second_person possessive.

it is a pronoun for third_person neutral.

this is a pronoun for demonstrative.

that is a pronoun for demonstrative.

we is a pronoun for first_person plural.

am is a verb for be present first_person.

are is a verb for be present.

is is a verb for be present.

be is a verb for be base.

can is a verb for modal ability.

do is a verb for auxiliary action.

does is a verb for auxiliary action.

did is a verb for auxiliary past.

have is a verb for auxiliary possession.

has is a verb for auxiliary possession.

use is a verb for action.

ask is a verb for action.

answer is a verb for action.

explain is a verb for action.

talk is a verb for action.

chat is a verb for action.

type is a verb for action.

run is a verb for action.

running is a verb for action progressive.

mean is a verb for definition.

means is a verb for definition.

what is a question for word.

who is a question for word.

where is a question for word.
